intro: C targets low-level hardware structures, requiring manual pointer tracking and explicit platform compilation configurations to operate securely. Dart delivers a modern, object-oriented, client-optimized application language featuring solid sound type checking, automated garbage collections, and multi-platform compilation paths. Migrating software designs from C to Dart empowers developers to safely scale cross-platform user interfaces and asynchronous network components.

diffs.from: Static, loose type rules permitting arbitrary pointer conversions and memory structural overlaps.
diffs.to: Static, sound type system featuring robust type inference and strict compile-time null safety validations.

diffs.from: Procedural syntax separating layouts across distinct header layouts and function targets.
diffs.to: Modern, class-based object-oriented structure resembling Java/C# with native functional additions.

diffs.from: Manual application-driven allocations managed using allocator pointers (`malloc`/`free`).
diffs.to: Automatic memory allocation pipelines leveraging a generational garbage collector engine.

diffs.from: Low-level multi-threading operations using custom operating system process hooks.
diffs.to: First-class single-threaded event loops supported by explicit `Future` interfaces and `async/await` syntax.

diffs.from: Basic operating system bridges tracking binary data formats and standard file descriptors.
diffs.to: Comprehensive cross-platform libraries tracking collection structures, HTTP operations, and string encodings.

examples.title: Asynchronous Operations
examples.description: Replacing manual thread blocking operations with clean async event-driven promises.

examples.title: Collection Management
examples.description: Replacing raw sized buffer pointers with dynamic managed lists.

tips.title: Leverage Sound Null Safety Rules
tips.body: Enforce clear variable structures using trailing question marks (e.g., `String?`) to explicitly identify types that are permitted to contain null states.

tips.title: Isolate Heavy Compute Paths using Isolates
tips.body: Dart code executes inside an isolated single thread loop. For intensive calculations that might stall user interfaces, spawn explicit `Isolate` workers to run concurrent threads safely.

tips.title: Utilize FFI only for Legacy Extensions
tips.body: If you must reuse legacy low-level C libraries, integrate Dart's `dart:ffi` system to pass data across runtime boundaries without compromising general code structure.

tips.title: Use Cascades for Concise Object Configuration
tips.body: Simplify repetitive field assignments or sequential method calls on a single object instance by using Dart's double-dot operator syntax (`..`).
